rationale: The citing paper gives Berger and Fitzsimons (2008) a specific functional role in Study 4c by stating that its thought-listing accessibility measure is adapted from that prior work. This is more than a background mention because it informs how a key construct (category accessibility) is operationalized in the citing paper's empirical design. Although the citation is not the central theoretical base of the rabbit-hole framework, it materially supports measurement implementation.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Berger and Fitzsimons (2008) as a methodological precedent for measuring category accessibility via a thought-listing paradigm. It transfers that approach to a new context, media-consumption sequence effects.
